description: §19.9.8 / §19 — scrml has no `try`/`catch`/`finally`; the error model is `fail` / `?` / `!{}` / `<errorBoundary>`. The DEFAULT parse path (block-splitter + ast-builder) fires the parse-layer rejection E-TRY-NOT-IN-SCRML, matching the opt-in `--parser=scrml-native` path (parse-stmt.js:parseTry). Fork-D drain-path-1 backport: previously the default path SQUATTED on E-ERROR-007 (which §19.10.4 reserves for nested-`transaction` blocks); `notCodes` asserts the squat is gone. (The separate W-TRY-CATCH-IN-SCRML-SOURCE warning still fires as an advisory sibling.)
rationale: §19.9.8: scrml has no `try`/`catch`/`finally`. A source-level `try` is a hard parse-layer rejection: E-TRY-NOT-IN-SCRML.
